programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Collage
programs.description:
Explore
collage as a rich, intuitive way to build images and tell stories in this
five-week workshop. You’ll work with a variety of materials while developing a
stronger understanding of composition, colour theory and how visual elements
can create narrative. Experiment with arranging, layering and editing imagery
while learning how colour relationships, balance and contrast guide the
viewer’s eye and shape meaning. For all levels and experience.

programs.name: Colour Theory 101
programs.description: Explore the  science and art of colour through mixing hues, tints, shades and tones. Practices  in accurate colour matching and achieving colour balance and harmony will be  demonstrated. By the end of the course, students will learn the methods of  constructing a cohesive colour palette and the importance of working within a  selective colour spectrum.

programs.name: Community Leadership After-School Program (CLASP)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? Community Leadership After School Program (CLASP) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

This camp provides an opportunity to make friends when participating in arts, games and sports activities while reducing barriers. For more information call 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational with Caregiver
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Stroke Improvement
programs.description: Work on stroke correction and improving technique through drills and progressions focused on front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.
